preamble: Assibilation of [g] to the sibilant [s] (often via an affricate stage) is a historical process found in the development of Romance and other language families.

examples.note: Latin /g/ before front vowel → Old French [dʒ] → Modern French [ʒ]. NOTE: the result is voiced [ʒ], not [s]; this example illustrates the g→ʒ chain rather than direct g→s.
